---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: e13adcbacdfdba1b8ce95d9bbb311a48f5eb3239
findings:
  - { id: judged-wizard-display-conventions-axis, kind: judged, text: "AC-1 states the --wizard interview 'walks vocabulary renames, template-set selection, AND display conventions with live validation preview' — three distinct configurable axes. The implementation (internal/initwizard/interview.go) walks only two configurable axes: vocabulary renames and template-set selection. 'Display conventions' (pluralization/capitalization/article) are not a separately-interviewed axis; they are auto-derived from the vocabulary display words (model.go pluralizeDisplay/DisplayClassPlural), and the interview's own doc comment explicitly frames the design as 'two configurable axes.' Functionally coherent, but the spec's third named axis is subsumed into vocabulary rather than surfaced as its own walked, previewed step — a reading a graph diff cannot see. (confidence 0.34)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Coherent design: the wizard walks two configurable axes (vocabulary renames, template-set selection); display conventions (pluralization/capitalization/article) are AUTO-DERIVED from the vocabulary display words (model.go pluralizeDisplay/DisplayClassPlural), not a separate interviewed step. The frozen feature ac-1's 'three axes' framing is imprecise — two walked + one derived-from-the-first. Behavior correct; queued for ratification with the init-wizard cluster (08-revision-notes). Honesty slice." }
  - { id: judged-reaffirmations-kind-audit-counting, kind: judged, text: "AC-5 says the waive workflow's reaffirmation flow and audit counting operate 'over the waivers/ AND reaffirmations/ kinds the model already carries.' The implementation's --reaffirm does not mint a reaffirmations/\u003cslug\u003e/\u003cid\u003e.md artifact of that kind; it appends a dated entry to an in-body reaffirmation log inside the existing waiver file, and audit counts only active waivers — the reaffirmations/ kind is never written or counted by this feature. This is a disclosed, ratified reading (recorded in the verb-surfaces spec's 'Disclosed reading' section and ledger L-N9, where reaffirmations/ refers to the pre-existing v0 content-hash reaffirmation kind), not a silent gap — but the literal AC-5 text names reaffirmations/ as a kind this flow acts over, and the implementation diverges from that wording by construction. (confidence 0.30)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "The disclosed, story-level-ratified reading (verb-surfaces spec 'Disclosed reading' + ledger L-N9): --reaffirm re-confirms through the waiver record (an in-body dated log), because the pre-existing reaffirmations/ kind's schema needs an Old!=New AC-text pair a waive reaffirmation lacks; audit counts waiver freshness. The judge accepted this at the story level (0 findings there). The frozen feature ac-5 naming reaffirmations/ as a kind the waive flow writes is imprecise by construction; queued for ratification. Honesty slice, not fix-class." }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "All five audits are complete. The implementation is highly faithful; ACs 2, 3, and 4 are fully satisfied with genuine parity/witness proofs. Two semantic reading-divergences from the literal AC text survive as low-confidence findings.\n\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"wizard-display-conventions-axis\",\"text\":\"AC-1 states the --wizard interview 'walks vocabulary renames, template-set selection, AND display conventions with live validation preview' — three distinct configurable axes. The implementation (internal/initwizard/interview.go) walks only two configurable axes: vocabulary renames and template-set selection. 'Display conventions' (pluralization/capitalization/article) are not a separately-interviewed axis; they are auto-derived from the vocabulary display words (model.go pluralizeDisplay/DisplayClassPlural), and the interview's own doc comment explicitly frames the design as 'two configurable axes.' Functionally coherent, but the spec's third named axis is subsumed into vocabulary rather than surfaced as its own walked, previewed step — a reading a graph diff cannot see.\",\"confidence\":0.34},{\"id\":\"reaffirmations-kind-audit-counting\",\"text\":\"AC-5 says the waive workflow's reaffirmation flow and audit counting operate 'over the waivers/ AND reaffirmations/ kinds the model already carries.' The implementation's --reaffirm does not mint a reaffirmations/\u003cslug\u003e/\u003cid\u003e.md artifact of that kind; it appends a dated entry to an in-body reaffirmation log inside the existing waiver file, and audit counts only active waivers — the reaffirmations/ kind is never written or counted by this feature. This is a disclosed, ratified reading (recorded in the verb-surfaces spec's 'Disclosed reading' section and ledger L-N9, where reaffirmations/ refers to the pre-existing v0 content-hash reaffirmation kind), not a silent gap — but the literal AC-5 text names reaffirmations/ as a kind this flow acts over, and the implementation diverges from that wording by construction.\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}" }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-wizard-display-conventions-axis** [accepted-deviation]: AC-1 states the --wizard interview 'walks vocabulary renames, template-set selection, AND display conventions with live validation preview' — three distinct configurable axes. The implementation (internal/initwizard/interview.go) walks only two configurable axes: vocabulary renames and template-set selection. 'Display conventions' (pluralization/capitalization/article) are not a separately-interviewed axis; they are auto-derived from the vocabulary display words (model.go pluralizeDisplay/DisplayClassPlural), and the interview's own doc comment explicitly frames the design as 'two configurable axes.' Functionally coherent, but the spec's third named axis is subsumed into vocabulary rather than surfaced as its own walked, previewed step — a reading a graph diff cannot see. (confidence 0.34) — Coherent design: the wizard walks two configurable axes (vocabulary renames, template-set selection); display conventions (pluralization/capitalization/article) are AUTO-DERIVED from the vocabulary display words (model.go pluralizeDisplay/DisplayClassPlural), not a separate interviewed step. The frozen feature ac-1's 'three axes' framing is imprecise — two walked + one derived-from-the-first. Behavior correct; queued for ratification with the init-wizard cluster (08-revision-notes). Honesty slice.
- **judged-reaffirmations-kind-audit-counting** [accepted-deviation]: AC-5 says the waive workflow's reaffirmation flow and audit counting operate 'over the waivers/ AND reaffirmations/ kinds the model already carries.' The implementation's --reaffirm does not mint a reaffirmations/<slug>/<id>.md artifact of that kind; it appends a dated entry to an in-body reaffirmation log inside the existing waiver file, and audit counts only active waivers — the reaffirmations/ kind is never written or counted by this feature. This is a disclosed, ratified reading (recorded in the verb-surfaces spec's 'Disclosed reading' section and ledger L-N9, where reaffirmations/ refers to the pre-existing v0 content-hash reaffirmation kind), not a silent gap — but the literal AC-5 text names reaffirmations/ as a kind this flow acts over, and the implementation diverges from that wording by construction. (confidence 0.30) — The disclosed, story-level-ratified reading (verb-surfaces spec 'Disclosed reading' + ledger L-N9): --reaffirm re-confirms through the waiver record (an in-body dated log), because the pre-existing reaffirmations/ kind's schema needs an Old!=New AC-text pair a waive reaffirmation lacks; audit counts waiver freshness. The judge accepted this at the story level (0 findings there). The frozen feature ac-5 naming reaffirmations/ as a kind the waive flow writes is imprecise by construction; queued for ratification. Honesty slice, not fix-class.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)
